mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand across consumption and investment channels, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict direction with a Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or housing starts decline while retail sales rise without affecting returns.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are uncorrelated with INDPRO momentum (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of monetary easing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5d returns in > 70% of instances when CPIAUCSL crosses the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal shows a negative or null relationship with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC <= 0.0) over a 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate profit margins and dividend payouts, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 revisions show no predictive power for 60-day equity returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 0.8 percent signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain bottlenecks, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.
